opp.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Important' and Merlin plus Merlin Intake are tagged on the opportunity, but the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all empty. The Merlin attachment is Zycus-driven, not a reflection of documented buyer commentary.
• The April 1 call (26 minutes) with Lisa Miller, Becca West and Anita Newsom produced action items strictly on Showpad creation, supplier-management module questions covering quality and spec docs, and demo recordings spanning intake-to-pay, supplier performance management, project management and sourcing. Merlin, agentic AI and autonomous negotiation were not raised by the buyer.
• The May 12 follow-up was a short 18-minute session and also produced no AI signal. The buyer's focus is on confirming module functionality, especially spec document storage, and consuming information via Showpad. With no buyer AI quotes and the commentary fields blank, this lands at Curious with low confidence.

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case has been articulated by the buyer across the three read calls. The pain is clear and qualitative: on 12 May 2026 Vatricia McClurg described the core problem as lack of centralisation, saying her team is 'managing a lot in our emails, and then everybody is at different paces', and flagged downstream tail-spend leakage where 'someone has committed... gotten commitment from a supplier... without any approval flow'. Becca West (1 Apr 2026) cited a clunky NetSuite integration and a UI where 'the PO isn't tied to the invoice'. None of this was converted into dollars, hours saved, or cycle-time targets. The move is to build a quantified savings/efficiency model with Vatricia's finance lead before the 16 Jul RFP response so the business case is explicit in the proposal.

champion.narrative: Anita Newsom (Senior Director of Procurement) is the entry champion: she recommended and onboarded Zycus at Ventura Foods and a prior construction company, reconnected with Zycus at ProcureCon and made the introduction ('I recommended and onboarded Zycus at Ventura Foods... it kind of reminded me of you all', 1 Apr 2026). That is genuine advocacy. What has developed: she opened the door and put Zycus in the procurement RFP. What is still missing: she has not appeared on a call since 1 Apr (~12 weeks) and is absent from recent Next Step entries; day-to-day ownership has shifted to Vatricia McClurg, who is engaged but reads as a neutral evaluator, not yet an advocate. The risk is Anita's sponsorship fades while Vatricia runs a competitive RFP. The move is to re-engage Anita to coach the RFP response and convert Vatricia into an active champion.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent being evaluated for displacement on renewal; Becca West (1 Apr 2026) said 'we're on Coupa' and named the wedge for us: poor UI, PO not tied to invoice, and a clunky NetSuite integration. The genuine alternative is 'Do Nothing / stay on Coupa', which is live since Vatricia (2 Jun, per Next Step) said they were deciding and the rep offered to 'get competitive with pricing'. LinkSquares is the buyer's CLM and is an integration requirement, not a procurement competitor; Zycus positioned its own CLM as an optional alternative on 12 May. We win by leaning into the integration and simplicity wedge (NetSuite + LinkSquares + Teams, midsize-appropriate) where Coupa frustrates them, and by quantifying tail-spend savings. No other named procurement vendor appears in any source.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific, confirmed and owned by procurement. Vatricia McClurg (12 May 2026) owns it: a manual, email-and-document-driven procurement process with no centralised hub, made worse as the team scales into indirect spend, plus tail-spend leakage from off-process commitments ('we don't know who that is, where it's come from... without any approval flow'). Becca West (1 Apr) owns the systems pain: Coupa's poor UI, PO not tied to invoice across multiple screens, and a clunky NetSuite integration. The 22 May demo surfaced 'digital fragmentation' across disconnected tools with low adoption. Inferred (not stated): the Coupa renewal is the compelling event driving urgency. Pain owner is clearly the growing procurement function under Anita/Vatricia.

paper_process.narrative: The contracting mechanics are unknown. Becca West (1 Apr 2026) referenced an NDA gate: 'I put together a requirements document... if we move further... and we have an NDA signed, we can share that'. Beyond that NDA reference there is no evidence of MSA/SOW path, legal review, procurement channel, or InfoSec process. Manscaped has multiple legal contacts on the account (Richard Protzmann, Kody Diaz, Cameron Westcott) but none has engaged on this opp. The contract-expiration of the incumbent Coupa is a likely forcing function (renewal cited as the trigger) but its date is not on record. Visibility requires asking Vatricia for the post-decision paper path and timeline now, given only 15 days sit between the 16 Aug decision and the 31 Aug close.

sources: SF buyer contact roles (Lisa Miller, Becca West)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has surfaced on any call. All three conversations were mid-level: Anita Newsom (Senior Director of Procurement, the referral source), Becca West (Director of Financial & Accounting Systems), Lisa Miller (Purchasing Manager) and Vatricia McClurg (procurement project owner, 'POC'). At a $130k ACV a director-level group can often approve, but no budget owner or signatory has been named or engaged, and the approval chain is undocumented. Account-level contacts who could be the EB (Eric Lam, SVP Finance & Accounting) have not appeared on a call for THIS opportunity. The move is for the deal owner to ask Vatricia directly who signs and what the approval path is, and to seek an introduction to the finance budget owner before the 16 Aug decision.

sources: SF Next Step history 27 Mar - 29 Jun 2026
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, staged RFP-driven process and the call sequence maps it: intro at ProcureCon (Apr) via Anita Newsom's referral, a first AP-focused RFP (Zycus excluded from AP, included for procurement), then a procurement RFP, RFP/Discovery (12 May), procurement demo (22 May), and on 29 Jun a re-scoped RFP splitting Procurement-Only vs P2P with responses due 16 Jul and a decision due 16 Aug. The process is procurement-led (Vatricia owns it; Lisa and Becca participate). What is missing is the back-half: who approves after the 16 Aug recommendation, and what the contracting path looks like. The re-issued RFP is the key forcing function to engage now.

sources: Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026 (Anita Newsom, Becca West)
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well documented from the buyer's own words. On 12 May 2026 Vatricia McClurg named: a user-friendly, simple solution 'tailored for smaller/midsize companies' (not 'too big or complex'); a centralised hub to replace email-driven vendor onboarding, RFP/RFQ and NDA tracking; the ability to attach/store supplier documents; reporting to compare bids; tracking POs after invoice date without needing PO entry; and critical integrations with NetSuite (ERP) and LinkSquares (CLM). Anita Newsom (1 Apr) added analytics on RFP responses and supplier quality/SOP document storage. AI is a 'nice to have once the basics are in place' per Vatricia (12 May). The 22 May demo confirmed positive reception of the Teams integration and supplier risk monitoring ('super cool'). Simplicity and integration fit are the decisive criteria.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake and the Autonomous Negotiation Agent (Anna) were demonstrated and received positively, but the buyer explicitly ranked AI as a later 'nice to have' behind getting the basics centralised. The agentic story is landing as future value, not the core decision driver, so it is under-leveraged as a differentiator versus Coupa.

items.quote: we aren't looking for something that's too big or complex for us because right now, we're not at that phase
items.criterion: Simple, midsize-appropriate procurement solution (not too big/complex)

items.quote: do you guys integrate with Netsuite pretty... we do use Linksquares
items.criterion: NetSuite ERP and LinkSquares CLM integration

items.quote: we would like kind of a centralized place to have all those items so then tracking downstream is a little bit easier
items.criterion: Centralised hub for vendor onboarding, RFP/NDA tracking, bid comparison

items.quote: Shared updated pricing to reflect limited scope

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the buyer's stated criteria of simplicity, centralisation and integration. Zycus matches the NetSuite + LinkSquares + Teams integration needs and the midsize-appropriate procurement hub; the main exposure is proving simplicity (not over-complex) and pricing for the narrowed Procurement-Only scope.

hard.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Important' and Merlin plus Merlin Intake are tagged on the opportunity, but the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all empty. The Merlin attachment is Zycus-driven, not a reflection of documented buyer commentary.
• The April 1 call (26 minutes) with Lisa Miller, Becca West and Anita Newsom produced action items strictly on Showpad creation, supplier-management module questions covering quality and spec docs, and demo recordings spanning intake-to-pay, supplier performance management, project management and sourcing. Merlin, agentic AI and autonomous negotiation were not raised by the buyer.
• The May 12 follow-up was a short 18-minute session and also produced no AI signal. The buyer's focus is on confirming module functionality, especially spec document storage, and consuming information via Showpad. With no buyer AI quotes and the commentary fields blank, this lands at Curious with low confidence.
